title: Create Viz
use_when: Create publication-quality visualizations with Python. Use when turning query results or a DataFrame into a chart, selecting the right chart type for a trend or comparison, generating a plot for a report or presentation, or creating an interactive chart with hover and zoom.
do_not_use_when: Do not use Create Viz when the request is mainly a different lifecycle artifact; route to the narrower Productize skill that owns that artifact instead. Do not use it when the user lacks enough context to distinguish facts, assumptions, and decision risk.
output_artifact: Create Viz UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
framework_fit: Create Viz fits Analytics work in the Design lifecycle when the user needs Create Viz UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks and has enough product context to make tradeoffs explicit. Use it to produce a decision-ready artifact, not a framework tour.
failure_modes: Produces Create Viz UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks without tying recommendations to the user's Design stage, evidence, and decision pressure.
Treats Create Viz as generic Analytics advice instead of naming assumptions, missing inputs, risks, and the next validation step.
Fails to route away from Create Viz when another Productize skill owns the requested artifact more directly.

examples.prompt: Use $create-viz to turn this create context into a decision-ready Create Viz UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks.
examples.expected_artifact: Create Viz UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
